methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Velaura AI is a Series C-stage semiconductor company founded by Rajiv Khemani that has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 private and 3 public peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including same-stage and adjacent-stage companies like DEEPX, SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and Positron AI.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $600M total raised as a proxy valuation and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $1.0B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—SambaNova ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—produces a median of $1.9B, with a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable; while the Semiconductors sector shows a 20.6x median price-to-sales across three public tickers, Velaura's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $867M to $2.1B, with a median of $1.4B across the two applicable methods. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations (reflecting stage and technology differentiation within the sector) and the absence of revenue visibility, which prevents direct public-market anchoring. Stage progression and peer positioning suggest the $1.0B–$1.9B band is most defensible.
